mutations.description: Accept a standard CAN identifier above the documented 11-bit maximum.
Accept a payload length above the classic CAN eight-byte bound.
Start a transmission without acknowledging stale terminal TX status.
Program the transmit mailbox with a fixed identifier instead of the caller frame.
Program a frame but omit the TX terminal interrupt enable.
Allow a new TX request before its previous terminal result is consumed.
Observe IRQ status without acknowledging exactly the observed bits.
Ignore a simultaneous TX error and report completion instead.
Report a TX error without returning its hardware error flag.
Read a received frame but fail to make it pending for the foreground consumer.
Drain an occupied receive mailbox without recording the software-slot overflow.
Keep the CAN controller active after a bus-off terminal IRQ.
Enter bus-off state without publishing the terminal result to foreground code.
Reconfigure the controller while hardware still reports bus-off or no recovery-ready indication.
Modify TX state without first masking the non-nested CAN IRQ.
mutations.find:
const uint32_t previous_interrupt_state = can0_irq_save_disable();
  bool sent = false;
mutations.replace:
const uint32_t previous_interrupt_state = 0u;
  bool sent = false;

mutations.description: Re-enable interrupts after taking a received frame even when they were already masked.
